changes.exact_quote: Upon payment, Vendor assigns Customer 75% of the right, title, and interest in project-specific deliverables. For embedded pre-existing materials, Vendor grants a perpetual license sufficient for Customer to use the deliverables. Customer has 15 days to identify delivery defects, and Vendor updates the embedded-material inventory within 40 days after a release.
changes.proposed_redline: Vendor assigns to Customer all right, title, and interest in project-specific deliverables upon payment, and grants Customer a perpetual license to any embedded pre-existing materials necessary to use those deliverables.

changes.exact_quote: Vendor will maintain insurance it determines is commercially reasonable, including general liability coverage of at least $250,000 per occurrence if available on commercially reasonable terms.
changes.proposed_redline: Vendor will maintain commercially reasonable insurance at its own expense, including technology errors and omissions/cyber liability coverage of at least $5,000,000 per claim and commercial general liability coverage of at least $2,000,000 per occurrence.

changes.exact_quote: For purposes of this clause, the following additional term applies: Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.
changes.proposed_redline: Despite any contrary sentence, the limitation in Section 16 does not apply to claims under Section 13 or Section 14.

changes.exact_quote: Vendor may amend any legal or commercial term by posting revised terms on its website, effective immediately without Customer approval.
changes.proposed_redline: No amendment, modification, or waiver is effective unless in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties.

summary: All eight T2-N response children reviewed against the v4 answer key.
